audit_items.proof_trace: checkUpkeep determines eligibility using (feeAggregatorBalance_T0 * oraclePrice_T0 / decimals) >= minAuctionSizeUsd and encodes the T0 balance as the amount. performUpkeep re-fetches oracle prices with withValidation=true (L315, L342) and recomputes (amount_T0 * oraclePrice_T1 / decimals); if the price drops between check and perform such that this falls below minAuctionSizeUsd, it reverts with AmountBelowMinAuctionSize (L346-348). Because performUpkeep has no per-asset try/catch, a revert on any single eligible asset also aborts the entire endedAuctions loop (L359-369), blocking pending auction closes.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An asset barely above minAuctionSizeUsd threshold is returned as eligible by checkUpkeep; by the time the Chainlink Automation keeper's performUpkeep transaction executes, the oracle price has dropped (naturally or via adversarial feed manipulation), causing AmountBelowMinAuctionSize revert that aborts all auction starts and end-of-auction settlements in the same upkeep batch.
